floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Frontier top-3 cleared: do-while(0) wrap direction-invariant +2 insns (KILLS pri-lift lever), cnt-carrier regresses to masked 4, volatile fmt-carrier policy-rejected. h5 floor holds at 2.

s14 directed PERM_GENERAL on pointer-arith nodes: 23,427 iters / 895s / 6 new finds all >= baseline (3 novel @ score 40 g3-basin, 3 @ score 50); AssertionError blindspot on `(u8*)t0 + (s32)tbl_125c` s

frontier.hypothesis: Directed PERM_GENERAL constrained to alternatives that PRESERVE h5's mult-expander chassis (multi-set t0, fresh p106 SLL dest) reveals sub-60 forms that random alone misses.
frontier.mechanism: The s13/s14 h5-multexpander campaigns both saw base_score=60 (s13) or collapsed to 40 (s14, due to alternatives including g3-family). A directed-PERM alternative set restricted to h5-preserving spellings (e.g. only nested-cast variations that keep `t0 *= 4` -> mult-expand -> p106 fresh SLL dest, and only arg5-address decompositions that leave p107 single-SET so 121 keeps LAUNCH) would open the neighborhood the s7 forensics named. Requires vetting each alternative for h5-basin preservation before launch.
frontier.next_probe: Audit each PERM_GENERAL alternative against the s7-CONFIRMED h5-vs-g3 basin classification: `t0 *= 4` (MULT expand path) OR any equivalent that routes through expand_mult (preserves p106 fresh dest); reject any alternative that reaches expand_binop with target=p101 (that's the g3-basin trap). Restrict to 2-3 alternatives that provably preserve h5. Relaunch tmp/perm_csmd4 with the constrained annotation.

frontier.hypothesis: PERM_LINESWAP applied to the inline block's statement ordering discovers a novel schedule-space arrangement the s3 hand-swept 13 structural axes and s4-s5 wrap/carrier probes missed.
frontier.mechanism: PERM_LINESWAP enumerates all orderings of a specified line block; s3's 13 variants were hand-picked (decl order, split-variables, expression compression), NOT a complete enumeration of the ~8! = 40320 orderings of the 8 statements in the inline block. LINESWAP is dataflow-safe (permutes independent lines) and would be a systematic complement to the hand sweep.
frontier.next_probe: Add `PERM_LINESWAP(...)` around the 8-line block `t0 = idx_1494[0]; v0 = idx_1494[1]; pp = ...; t0 *= 4; t0 = ...; v0 <<= 2; arg5 = ...; debug_printf(...);` (or just the 6 non-CALL lines with the call held below). Launch a fresh h5-basin campaign with LINESWAP as the only PERM directive; expected mutation space ~O(n!) minus dataflow-invalid orderings. Complements the directed-PERM approach with systematic schedule-space coverage.

frontier.hypothesis: The decomp.me corpus scrape (still gated on curl_cffi install) surfaces a similar-asm scratch whose C shape lies OUTSIDE the h5/g3 basins entirely.
frontier.mechanism: Coarse asm-overlap pre-filter over the downloaded decomp.me corpus (BB2 toolchain class: gcc2.7.2-psx / gcc2.7.2-cdk / psyq3.5); manual inspection of top hits could reveal a novel-in-corpus C form the s1-s14 structural + permuter neighborhoods have not reached.
frontier.next_probe: Operator installs curl_cffi (`source .venv/bin/activate && pip install curl_cffi`); next rederive-modality session runs `python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py search --asm-file asm/funcs/cpu_side_move_dir_4.s` and inspects top asm-overlap hits.
